expectations.evidence: Several proposals omit one or more layers. Opportunity 2 (Industry Social Proof) explicitly states 'No AI generation needed here -- just use recalled properties to select from pre-built content variants.' Opportunity 6 (Smart Signup Flow) uses only recall for form pre-fill with no governance or generation. Opportunity 7 (Solution Cross-Links) uses recall + prompt but no smartGuidelines. Only Opportunities 1, 5, and 8 use all three layers.

Multiple proposals center on selecting/tagging rather than generating original content. Opportunity 2 explicitly says 'No AI generation needed here -- just use recalled properties to select from pre-built content variants. Fast and cheap.' Opportunity 3 selects from existing blog posts. Opportunity 6 pre-fills forms from recalled properties. Opportunity 7 recommends existing solution pages. These are CRM-style segmentation, not AI-generated content.

Opportunity 2 (Industry Social Proof) is described as 'a simple lookup rather than AI generation' -- this is basic CRM segmentation. Opportunity 6 (Smart Signup Flow) pre-fills form fields from recalled properties -- any CRM can do this. Opportunity 3 (Blog Recommendations) selects from existing posts based on interests -- standard recommendation engine behavior. These proposals do not require Personize's three-layer architecture.

Governance appears in a brief 'Governance Setup' section near the end with 4 generic bullet points (brand voice, personalization boundaries, compliance rules, fallback rules). It is not integrated into most individual proposals -- Opportunities 2, 3, 6, and 7 have no smartGuidelines call in their code. The text says guidelines are 'retrieved automatically' but most code examples do not include them, making governance feel optional rather than essential.

While the architecture describes three data feeds, most individual proposals use only a single data source. Opportunity 2 uses only the industry property. Opportunity 6 uses only contact recall for form pre-fill. Opportunity 7 uses only browsing history. The response does not consistently demonstrate how combining multiple sources unlocks capabilities impossible with any single source. Only Opportunity 5 (campaign landing pages) pulls both Contact and Company digests to show cross-source value.
